articles.title: Supreme Court Pulls Up NTA Over NEET 2026 Leak | Centre Says PM Modi Supervising Reforms
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Friday sharply questioned the Centre and the National Testing Agency over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy, raising concerns about accountability, institutional failures and the trauma caused to students and families. During the hearing, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta informed the bench that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is personally monitoring the re-examination and reform process to ensure there are no further lapses. The Centre told the Court that new security mechanisms have been introduced ahead of the rescheduled NEET-UG exam on June 21. The NTA informed the Court that sweeping structural reforms have been implemented, including mandatory CCTV checks, forensic analysis of surveillance footage, mock drills, emergency preparedness plans and strengthened coordination with police and intelligence agencies. The Court questioned how the leak occurred despite regular monitoring by the High Powered Committee and observed that accountability within the NTA remained unclear. Stressing the need for lasting institutional reforms, the bench directed the Ministry of Education to file a separate affidavit explaining how the NEET examination process would be institutionalised on a year-to-year basis to prevent future controversies. The matter will now be heard again in July.

articles.title: 'No Need To Sing Vande Matram In...' Pinarayi Vijayan Hits out at Centre, BJP After Row In Assembly
articles.description: Kerala opposition leader Pinarayi Vijayan has criticised the Centre and BJP over the Vande Mataram controversy in the state assembly. He alleged full rendition of the national song is part of RSS agenda and defended partial performance. BJP accused the assembly of insulting the national song during Governor Arlekar’s address. CPM and BJP exchanged sharp political attacks over protocol, secularism and historical usage. The state government clarified it had no role, saying the programme was organised by Lok Bhavan authorities.

articles.title: ₹1,000 Crore for Damage Control: The Real Cost of India's Exam Crisis
articles.description: The NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy and the CBSE Class 12 evaluation backlash may together cost India hundreds of crores in refunds, re-evaluations, operational expenses, and damage-control measures. Shreya Chandra examines the hidden financial burden of examination failures, the opportunity cost for India’s education system, and why recurring exam crises are becoming a national policy challenge.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Anne Frank: 'You can be lonely even when you are loved by many people, since you are still not anybody’s ‘one and only.’' - What The Diary of a Young Girl author teaches us about loneliness and emotional connection

articles.title: ‘Won’t Be Easy Against Turks’: Israeli-US Spy Predicts Future Conflicts Involving Turkey & Egypt
articles.description: Former Israeli-American spy Jonathan Pollard sparked controversy after claiming Israel must prepare for future wars beyond Iran. Speaking on an Arutz Sheva podcast, Pollard suggested Turkey and Egypt could become the next major conflict fronts and warned “the storm is coming.” His remarks arrive amid worsening Israel-Turkey tensions over Gaza and regional security concerns involving Syria. Despite long-standing diplomatic ties and peace agreements, relations between Israel, Turkey and Egypt have faced growing strain. #JonathanPollard #Israel #Turkey #Egypt #MiddleEast #Iran #Gaza #BreakingNews #Geopolitics #IsraelTurkey

articles.title: Siddaramaiah’s Exit May Haunt DK Shivakumar & Rahul Gandhi As Karnataka Caste Census Row Explodes
articles.description: Siddaramaiah’s decision to formally accept Karnataka’s caste census report just before resigning as Chief Minister has triggered intense political debate. The move is being seen as a calculated final message to both his likely successor DK Shivakumar and Congress leader Rahul Gandhi.The caste survey, pending for years because of its politically sensitive findings, reportedly highlights the growing demographic strength of OBCs, minorities, and Dalits under Siddaramaiah’s AHINDA political framework. But the report also threatens to reopen Karnataka’s delicate caste equations involving Lingayats and Vokkaligas.For DK Shivakumar, the caste census could become a major political challenge. For Rahul Gandhi, it has become a credibility test for the Congress party’s national caste census campaign.

articles.title: Delhi HC seeks response from Centre, X over Cockroach Janta Party's handle being withheld
articles.description: The Delhi High Court has issued notices to the Union government and X regarding the blocking of the Cockroach Janta Party's account. The satirical outfit's founder challenged the action, alleging it was based on national security grounds and raising concerns about online censorship and free speech.

articles.title: HR offered a 100% hike to candidate willing to accept lower pay; speechless, he learns it wasn’t just about him being the ‘strongest candidate’
articles.description: A viral X post about a job interview is sparking conversations around toxic workplaces, salary negotiations, and ethical hiring practices. The story follows a candidate who agreed to take a lower salary just to escape a bad work environment, only to be surprised with a much higher offer from the interviewer.

articles.title: Supreme Court refuses to interfere with anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati in POCSO case
articles.description: The Supreme Court has declined to intervene in the Allahabad High Court's decision to grant anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati in the case involving allegations under the POCSO Act. The High Court had previously granted bail to the Swami and his disciple. Both parties were directed to cooperate with the investigation and refrain from media statements.

articles.title: Terror, Pakistan, Hormuz And UNSC: NSA Ajit Doval Shares Blunt Message To The World From Russia
articles.description: India’s NSA Ajit Doval delivered one of his toughest global security speeches yet at the first international security forum in Moscow. From Pakistan-sponsored terrorism to the Iran war, Hormuz blockade fears and the growing irrelevance of the United Nations, Doval fired warnings at multiple fronts simultaneously. He slammed double standards on terrorism, warned against using terror as state policy and questioned whether outdated global institutions can still handle modern crises. Doval also highlighted the massive risks of a wider Iran conflict and disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, through which nearly one-fifth of the world’s oil passes. The speech signalled a sharper, more assertive India willing to openly challenge Pakistan, question the UNSC structure and defend its strategic interests in an increasingly unstable world order. Moscow became the stage for India’s strongest security message yet on terrorism, global instability and geopolitical hypocrisy.

articles.title: Helen Mirren called 'evil Zionist b****' in viral clip on night out with husband Taylor Hackford - WATCH
articles.description: Veteran actress Helen Mirren faced a viral social media incident where a Pro-Palestine protestor verbally accosted her and her husband, filmmaker Taylor Hackford, on a London street. The man, filming the couple, hurled accusations regarding Mirren's pro-Israel stance, referencing comments she made while promoting her film 'Golda'. The incident is reportedly being investigated as a potential hate crime.
